node.title: Initialize Next.js Project
node.description: Create a new Next.js 14 application with App Router, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. This will be the foundation of our e-commerce store.
node.expected_output: New Next.js project scaffolded in ./ecommerce-store directory with all dependencies installed

node.title: Install Core Dependencies
node.description: Install essential packages: stripe for payments, zustand for state management, lucide-react for icons, and zod for validation.
node.expected_output: All npm packages installed and ready to use
node.risks: Version conflicts are possible but unlikely with modern packages

node.title: Choose Database Solution
node.description: Select the database technology for storing products, orders, and user data

branch.label: Prisma + PostgreSQL
branch.pros: Type-safe queries, migrations, excellent DX, scales well
branch.cons: Requires PostgreSQL setup, slightly more complex

branch.label: Drizzle + SQLite
branch.pros: Zero config, great for prototypes, fast local dev

branch.label: Supabase
branch.pros: Managed service, real-time features, auth built-in

node.title: Set up Prisma with PostgreSQL
node.description: Initialize Prisma, configure connection, and create initial schema for products, users, and orders

node.title: Set up Drizzle with SQLite
node.description: Initialize Drizzle ORM with SQLite, create schema for products, users, and orders
node.expected_output: Drizzle configured with local SQLite database

node.title: Configure Supabase Connection
node.description: Set up Supabase client, configure authentication, and create database tables

dynamic_field.name: supabase_url
dynamic_field.title: Supabase URL
dynamic_field.setup_instructions: Find at app.supabase.com → Project Settings → API

dynamic_field.name: supabase_anon_key
dynamic_field.title: Supabase Anon Key
dynamic_field.setup_instructions: Find at app.supabase.com → Project Settings → API

node.title: Create Product Schema and Seed Data
node.description: Define the product model with fields: id, name, description, price, image, category, inventory. Create seed script with sample products.
node.expected_output: Product table with 10 sample products seeded
node.risks: Image URLs need to be valid and accessible

node.title: Build Product Listing Page
node.description: Create the main product grid showing all products with images, names, and prices. Include category filtering and sorting options.

node.title: Build Product Detail Page
node.description: Create individual product pages with full description, multiple images, size/variant selection, and add-to-cart button.

node.title: Implement Shopping Cart
node.description: Create a persistent shopping cart using Zustand with localStorage. Include add, remove, update quantity, and clear cart functionality.
node.expected_output: Working cart with slide-out drawer UI

node.title: Configure Stripe
node.description: Set up Stripe client, configure API keys, and create webhook endpoint for payment events.
node.expected_output: Stripe SDK configured and webhook handler ready

dynamic_field.name: stripe_secret_key
dynamic_field.title: Stripe Secret Key
dynamic_field.setup_instructions: Get from dashboard.stripe.com → Developers → API keys

dynamic_field.name: stripe_publishable_key
dynamic_field.title: Stripe Publishable Key
dynamic_field.description: Your Stripe publishable key (starts with pk_)
dynamic_field.setup_instructions: Get from dashboard.stripe.com → Developers → API keys

dynamic_field.name: stripe_webhook_secret
dynamic_field.title: Stripe Webhook Secret
dynamic_field.description: Webhook signing secret (optional for local dev)
dynamic_field.setup_instructions: Get when setting up webhooks in Stripe dashboard

node.title: Build Checkout Flow
node.description: Create checkout page with shipping form, integrate Stripe Elements for payment, and handle successful/failed payments.
node.expected_output: Complete checkout flow with Stripe payment form
node.risks: Payment integration requires careful error handling

node.title: Create Order Confirmation
node.description: Build order success page showing order details, estimated delivery, and confirmation email trigger.

node.title: Choose Authentication Method
node.description: Select how customers will sign in to the store

branch.label: NextAuth.js
branch.description: Flexible auth with OAuth and credentials
branch.pros: Multiple providers, session management, widely used

branch.label: Clerk
branch.pros: Beautiful UI, easy setup, user management dashboard

branch.label: Guest Checkout Only
branch.cons: No order history, no saved carts

node.title: Set up NextAuth.js
node.description: Configure NextAuth with Google OAuth and email/password credentials. Create login and register pages.

node.title: Integrate Clerk
node.description: Set up Clerk provider, create sign-in/sign-up components, and configure middleware for protected routes.

node.title: Add Loading States and Error Handling
node.description: Implement skeleton loaders for all pages, toast notifications for actions, and error boundaries for graceful failure handling.
node.expected_output: Polished UX with proper feedback for all states

node.title: Implement SEO and Metadata
node.description: Add dynamic meta tags for all pages, Open Graph images for social sharing, and structured data for products.
node.expected_output: SEO-optimized pages with proper meta tags

node.title: Performance Optimization
node.description: Optimize images with next/image, implement lazy loading, add caching headers, and run Lighthouse audit.

node.title: Deploy to Vercel
node.description: Configure environment variables, deploy to Vercel, set up custom domain, and configure Stripe webhook endpoint.

dynamic_field.name: domain
dynamic_field.title: Custom Domain
dynamic_field.description: Optional custom domain for the store
